notes: This verse is the chapter's classical interpretive crux on Veda-scope and the apparent nitya-sattva-stha paradox. The engineering layer handles both correctly. (1) 'Trai-guṇya-viṣayāḥ vedāḥ' is correctly read as scoping (the Vedas' karma-kāṇḍa portion takes the three-guṇa-realm as its subject/addressees) — both Shankara's reading (worldly-life-domain as object) and Ramanuja's reading (guṇa-preponderant persons as addressees) are recorded. (2) The nistrai-guṇya / nitya-sattva-stha apparent paradox is preserved with explicit note: commentators read sattva here as 'reality' (Shankara) or as 'pure-sattva non-mixed stage' (Ramanuja) to resolve the contradiction. The engineering layer does not pretend to resolve the metaphysical paradox; it explicitly tags the analog STRETCHED in scope and confidence MEDIUM. The transliterated terms (nistrai-guṇya, nirdvandva, nitya-sattva-stha, niryoga-kṣema, ātmavān, sattva, rajas, tamas) are all preserved load-bearing. Niryoga-kṣema is correctly glossed (acquisition-of-the-not-yet-had + preservation-of-the-already-had). PASS.
